note: Reported values are the report's PROPORTIONAL ('Disc') estimate. Tables 3-13 in the docx are embedded raster images; their numeric values were recovered from the authors' shipped result CSVs (Result_CSVs_2025data.zip), which are the digital source of those image tables and match every prose figure spot-checked (26.1/25.5/48.4, ecoregion & habitat composition, freshwater, finer habitats). Lower80/Upper20 = the report's lower/upper uncertainty band.

metric_definitions.percent_of_CA: share of California's official land area (~101.5M ac denominator)
metric_definitions.percent_of_feature: share of that feature's STATEWIDE extent lying within 30x30 (GAP 1+2), proportional estimate
metric_definitions.percent_of_network: share of the 30x30 network's area composed of that feature/ecoregion, proportional estimate

statistics.label: % of California in 30x30 Conservation Areas
statistics.definition: Percent of California's land area classified GAP 1+2 (durably protected for biodiversity), proportional estimate. Denominator = official CA area (~101.5M ac).
statistics.source: Exec summary / p.'Where Do We Stand' / Land Characterization; = CNRA 2025 progress report

statistics.label: % of California in other conserved/public/unknown lands (GAP 3+4)
statistics.definition: Percent of CA land classified GAP 3 or GAP 4 (other conservation, public, or unknown status).

statistics.label: % of California non-conserved
statistics.definition: Percent of CA land outside any conservation-area unit (private unmanaged + DOD).

statistics.label: Official 2025 30x30 Conservation Area acreage (GAP 1+2)
statistics.definition: Total statewide GAP 1+2 conserved acres in the 2025 Conserved Areas dataset.

statistics.label: Acreage increase since 2022
statistics.definition: Net new GAP 1+2 acreage added 2022->2025.

statistics.label: Acreage still needed to reach 30%
statistics.definition: Additional GAP 1+2 acres required to hit 30% of CA.

statistics.label: Network projected climate-stressed by mid-century
statistics.definition: % of the 30x30 network's natural vegetation projected to be stressed by mid-century climate change (Thorne et al. 2016).

statistics.label: Network at risk from sea-level rise
statistics.definition: % of the 30x30 network at risk from sea-level rise (NOAA 5 ft).

statistics.label: Network burned in past decade
statistics.definition: % of the 30x30 network burned by wildfire in the past decade (CAL FIRE).

statistics.label: Southeastern Great Basin share of 30x30 network
statistics.definition: Proportional % of the 30x30 network composed of the Southeastern Great Basin ecoregion.

statistics.definition: Proportional % of the 30x30 network composed of the Mojave Desert ecoregion.

statistics.label: BioRankSW
statistics.definition: ACE top-ranked biodiversity, statewide (Rank 5), CDFW 2018: % of extent within 30x30. Threshold feature (top quintile) - CA-level comparison N/A.

statistics.label: BioRankEco
statistics.definition: ACE top-ranked biodiversity, per-ecoregion (Rank 5): % of extent within 30x30. Threshold feature.

statistics.label: RarRankSW
statistics.definition: ACE top-ranked RARE biodiversity, statewide (Rank 5): % within 30x30. Threshold feature.

statistics.label: RarRankEco
statistics.definition: ACE top-ranked RARE biodiversity, per-ecoregion (Rank 5): % within 30x30. Threshold feature.

statistics.label: NtvRept
statistics.definition: Reptile richness, all native (top 20%), ACE/CDFW: % within 30x30.

statistics.label: NtvAmph
statistics.definition: Amphibian richness, all native (top 20%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: NtvMamm
statistics.definition: Mammal richness, all native (top 20%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: NtvBird
statistics.definition: Bird richness, all native (top 20%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: NtvPlnt
statistics.definition: Plant richness, all native (top 20%), ACE: % within 30x30.

statistics.label: RarRept
statistics.definition: Reptile richness, rare subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: RarAmph
statistics.definition: Amphibian richness, rare subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: RarMamm
statistics.definition: Mammal richness, rare subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: RarBird
statistics.definition: Bird richness, rare subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: RarPlnt
statistics.definition: Plant richness, rare subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: ReptEndem
statistics.definition: Reptile richness, endemic subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: AmphEndem
statistics.definition: Amphibian richness, endemic subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: MammEndem
statistics.definition: Mammal richness, endemic subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: BirdEndem
statistics.definition: Bird richness, endemic subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: PlntEndem
statistics.definition: Plant richness, endemic subset (top 5%): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: plant
statistics.definition: Plant species richness, top 20% (Kling et al. 2019): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: endp
statistics.definition: Rarity-weighted endemic plant richness, top 20% (Kling et al. 2019): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: chn
statistics.definition: Channelized connectivity (Cameron et al. 2022): % of extent within 30x30.

statistics.label: int
statistics.definition: Intensified connectivity (Cameron et al. 2022): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: diff
statistics.definition: Diffuse (well-connected) connectivity (Cameron et al. 2022): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: clink
statistics.definition: Climate migration routes / climate linkages (Schloss et al. 2022): % within 30x30.

statistics.label: scmlinkage
statistics.definition: Regional connectivity linkages (South Coast Missing Linkages, Beier et al. 2006): % of linkage extent within 30x30.

statistics.label: gde
statistics.definition: Groundwater-dependent ecosystems (Klausmeyer et al. 2018): % of GDE extent within 30x30. KEY FEATURE.

statistics.label: wetlands
statistics.definition: Wetlands = freshwater emergent + freshwater forested/shrub + estuarine & marine wetland (NWI 2019, 3 WETLAND_TYPE classes): % of wetland extent within 30x30. KEY FEATURE. NOTE: distinct from the FVEG CWHR13 'Wetland' habitat class.

statistics.label: fwa_rich
statistics.definition: Watersheds with the highest freshwater species richness (top, Howard et al. 2015): % of extent within 30x30.

statistics.label: stream_1_2
statistics.definition: Headwater streams (NHD order 1-2, USGS 2020): % of stream length within 30x30.

statistics.label: stream_3_5
statistics.definition: Mid-sized streams (NHD order 3-5): % of length within 30x30.

statistics.label: stream_6_9
statistics.definition: Major rivers (NHD order 6-9): % of length within 30x30.

statistics.label: stream_peren
statistics.definition: Perennial streams (NHD): % of length within 30x30.

statistics.label: flood
statistics.definition: 100-year floodplain / flood hazard zone (First Street Foundation 2020): % within 30x30. KEY FEATURE.

statistics.label: miroc
statistics.definition: Mid-century habitat climate exposure / projected climate stress (Thorne et al. 2016, MIROC): % of climate-stressed extent within 30x30; as %network the network share projected to be climate-stressed.

statistics.label: slr5ft
statistics.definition: Sea-level-rise inundation zone (NOAA, 5 ft): % of the SLR-exposed area within 30x30 (representation); as %network it is the network's SLR-at-risk share.

statistics.label: fire_perimeter
statistics.definition: Historic fire perimeters (CAL FIRE, past decade): % of burned-area extent within 30x30; as %network the share of the network burned in the past decade.
